title: M01-L003 — Pronouns and Who Questions

sections.heading: What you will be able to say after this lesson
body: Target runtime: 9-12 minutes
Scenario: You are at a small gathering. Someone points to a person across the room. You ask who they are, get an answer, and confirm.
Learner takeaway: You can now say I, you, he/she, point to someone and say who they are, and ask who someone is.

sections.heading: Core phrases (Thai / Transliteration / English)
body: ฉัน | chǎn | I (general)
ผม | phǒm | I (male, polite)
คุณ | khun | you
เขา | khǎo | he / she
นี่ | nîi | this
คือ | khuue | is (identity)
นี่คือ... | nîi khuue... | this is...
ใคร | khrai | who
เขาคือใคร | khǎo khuue khrai | who is he/she?
คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม | khun khuue...châi mǎi | you are...right?
ขอโทษ | khǎaw-thôot | excuse me / sorry
ประเทศ | prà-thêet | country
ไทย | thai | Thailand / Thai

sections.heading: Teaching notes and explanation cues
body: I and you — the first pronouns: Teach ฉัน, ผม, and คุณ as the building blocks for talking about people.
Let's start with the most important words in any conversation — I and you.
In Thai, there are different words for I. The general one is ฉัน chǎn. If you are male and want to sound polite, use ผม phǒm instead.
He, she, and this — pointing to people: Teach เขา, นี่, and คือ to build identity sentences.
Now let's talk about other people. เขา khǎo means he or she. Thai uses the same word for both.
นี่ nîi means this. And คือ khuue means is. So when you put them together: นี่คือ... nîi khuue... means this is...
Ask who someone is: Teach ใคร and the chunk เขาคือใคร to form who-questions.
Now the question word: ใคร khrai means who. In Thai, the question word stays where the answer would go.
So to ask who is he or she, you say: เขาคือใคร khǎo khuue khrai. Literally: he/she is who?
Put it together — a mini identity exchange: Combine all new pronouns and patterns with review vocabulary in a realistic mini-dialogue.
Let's use everything together. Imagine you are at a gathering and you see someone you don't know.
You turn to your friend and ask: เขาคือใคร khǎo khuue khrai — who is that?

sections.heading: Mini dialogues / role-play
body: You: ขอโทษครับ เขาคือใครครับ | khǎaw-thôot khráp khǎo khuue khrai khráp | Excuse me, who is he/she?
Friend: นี่คือแอนค่ะ | nîi khuue Anne khâ | This is Anne.
You: เขามาจากประเทศอะไรครับ | khǎo maa-jàak prà-thêet à-rai khráp | What country is she from?
Friend: เขามาจากประเทศไทยค่ะ | khǎo maa-jàak prà-thêet thai khâ | She's from Thailand.
You: คนไทยใช่ไหมครับ | khon thai châi mǎi khráp | A Thai person, right?
Friend: ใช่ค่ะ | châi khâ | Yes.

sections.heading: Practice drills (self-study)
body: Listen and repeat: ฉัน (chǎn), ผม (phǒm), คุณ (khun). Say each one twice.
Pause-and-produce: I say a situation — male speaker or female speaker. You say the right word for I before I show the answer.
Response-building: I say คุณชื่ออะไร (khun chûue à-rai). You answer with ฉันชื่อ... or ผมชื่อ... and your name.
Listen and repeat: เขา (khǎo), นี่ (nîi), คือ (khuue). Now together: นี่คือ... (nîi khuue...).
Substitution drill: I give a name. You say นี่คือ... with that name. Example: Mike → นี่คือไมค์.
Pause-and-produce: I point to someone and say เขาชื่ออะไร (khǎo chûue à-rai). You answer เขาชื่อ... with a name.
Listen and repeat: ใคร (khrai). Now the full question: เขาคือใคร (khǎo khuue khrai).
Response-building: I ask เขาคือใคร. You answer เขาคือ... with any name you choose.
Pause-and-produce: I say a name. You build the confirmation: คุณคือ[name]ใช่ไหม before I show it.
Role swap: I play the friend. You ask เขาคือใคร and I answer. Then you confirm with ใช่ไหม.
Substitution drill: I change the name and country. You rebuild the full exchange each time.
Production challenge: introduce yourself using ฉัน or ผม, then ask who the person next to you is.

sections.heading: Vocabulary list for review / flashcards
body: ฉัน | chǎn | I (general)
ผม | phǒm | I (male, polite)
คุณ | khun | you
เขา | khǎo | he / she
นี่ | nîi | this
คือ | khuue | is (identity)
นี่คือ... | nîi khuue... | this is...
ใคร | khrai | who
เขาคือใคร | khǎo khuue khrai | who is he/she?
คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม | khun khuue...châi mǎi | you are...right?
ขอโทษ | khǎaw-thôot | excuse me / sorry
ประเทศ | prà-thêet | country
ไทย | thai | Thailand / Thai

sections.heading: Quick recap + memory aids
body: How do you say I if you are male? ผม (phǒm).
How do you say this is...? นี่คือ... (nîi khuue...).
How do you ask who someone is? เขาคือใคร (khǎo khuue khrai).
How do you confirm someone's identity? คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม (khun khuue...châi mǎi).
What does คือ mean? It connects a person to their identity — is.

drills: Listen and repeat: ฉัน (chǎn), ผม (phǒm), คุณ (khun). Say each one twice.
Pause-and-produce: I say a situation — male speaker or female speaker. You say the right word for I before I show the answer.
Response-building: I say คุณชื่ออะไร (khun chûue à-rai). You answer with ฉันชื่อ... or ผมชื่อ... and your name.
Listen and repeat: เขา (khǎo), นี่ (nîi), คือ (khuue). Now together: นี่คือ... (nîi khuue...).
Substitution drill: I give a name. You say นี่คือ... with that name. Example: Mike → นี่คือไมค์.
Pause-and-produce: I point to someone and say เขาชื่ออะไร (khǎo chûue à-rai). You answer เขาชื่อ... with a name.
Listen and repeat: ใคร (khrai). Now the full question: เขาคือใคร (khǎo khuue khrai).
Response-building: I ask เขาคือใคร. You answer เขาคือ... with any name you choose.
Pause-and-produce: I say a name. You build the confirmation: คุณคือ[name]ใช่ไหม before I show it.
Role swap: I play the friend. You ask เขาคือใคร and I answer. Then you confirm with ใช่ไหม.
Substitution drill: I change the name and country. You rebuild the full exchange each time.
Production challenge: introduce yourself using ฉัน or ผม, then ask who the person next to you is.
answerKey: Use PTM inline transliteration marks in every practice line.
How do you say I if you are male? ผม (phǒm).
How do you say this is...? นี่คือ... (nîi khuue...).
How do you ask who someone is? เขาคือใคร (khǎo khuue khrai).
How do you confirm someone's identity? คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม (khun khuue...châi mǎi).
What does คือ mean? It connects a person to their identity — is.
